findings.summary: output_validator.py (validation layer) imports from infrastructure.project.discovery at function-level, creating a lateral dependency across unrelated subsystems
evidence: output_validator.py:200 — `from infrastructure.project.discovery import discover_projects` inside `validate_root_output_structure`
infrastructure/core/cli.py:184,284 — same deferred import pattern used for the same reason, suggesting the boundary is unclear
findings.suggestion: Pass project names as a parameter to `validate_root_output_structure` so the validation layer has no runtime dependency on the project discovery layer. Callers (cli.py, pipeline stages) already have access to discovered projects.

findings.summary: from typing import Dict/List/Tuple legacy imports persist in ~58 files across scientific/, publishing/, llm/, steganography/ while core/, validation/, and reporting/ have been migrated to builtin generics, creating a visible style island split
evidence: Grep for 'from typing import.*Dict|List|Tuple' returns 58 files — every file in scientific/, steganography/, publishing/, llm/ still uses legacy form
infrastructure/core/logging_utils.py uses `list[str]`, `dict[str, Any]` (builtin) throughout
infrastructure/reporting/pipeline_reporter.py uses `list[StageResult]`, `dict[str, Any]` (builtin) throughout
The split is not intentional variation; it reflects incomplete migration
findings.suggestion: Complete the migration: replace `Dict`, `List`, `Tuple` from `typing` with `dict`, `list`, `tuple` in all remaining files. A single sed pass or ruff rule (UP006, UP035) can enforce this.

findings.summary: audit_orchestrator.py swallows exceptions from multiple validators with only a warning log and no propagation, while sibling pipeline_reporter.py and integrity.py re-raise after logging
evidence: audit_orchestrator.py:76-78 — `except Exception as e: if verbose: logger.warning(...)` — exception silently dropped when verbose=False
audit_orchestrator.py:173-174 — `except Exception as e: logger.warning(f'Link validation failed...')` — no propagation, scan continues as if link validation succeeded
audit_orchestrator.py:195-196 — same pattern for each quality validator
pipeline_reporter.py:116-118 — `except OSError as e: logger.error(...); raise` — consistent re-raise pattern
integrity.py:69-71 — `except OSError as e: logger.error(...); integrity[...] = False` — result-based error signaling, also consistent within itself
findings.suggestion: Decide on a single strategy for recoverable validation errors: either record them in the result object (like integrity.py does) or accumulate them and raise at the end. The current per-validator silent swallow means callers cannot distinguish 'no issues found' from 'validation failed to run'.

findings.summary: output_validator.py returns False on critical missing files while pipeline_reporter.py raises OSError — inconsistent failure signaling across the same reporting subsystem
evidence: output_validator.py:113-125 — missing combined PDF sets `validation_passed = False` and returns False without raising
output_validator.py:169 — missing expected directory only logs a warning, returns True
pipeline_reporter.py:116-118 — `except OSError as e: logger.error(...); raise` — raises on write failure
pipeline_reporter.py:403-404 — `except OSError as e: logger.error(...); raise` — raises on validation markdown write failure
findings.suggestion: Standardize: validation functions that check pre-conditions (does output exist?) should consistently return a result object or bool; I/O functions that write files should consistently raise. Do not mix the two strategies in functions with similar names (`validate_*`) in the same subsystem.

findings.summary: pipeline_reporter.py and error_aggregator.py each implement an independent markdown error-report generator with nearly identical structure, creating duplicated logic with no shared abstraction
evidence: pipeline_reporter.py:487 — `def generate_error_markdown(summary: dict[str, Any]) -> str` — free function operating on raw dict

error_aggregator.py:224 — `def _generate_markdown_report(self, summary: dict[str, Any]) -> str` — private method with identical structure: header, errors-by-type section, error-details section, 10-item truncation
Both produce a '# Error Summary' Markdown report from the same dict shape; neither calls the other
findings.suggestion: Extract a single `render_error_summary_markdown(summary: dict[str, Any]) -> str` into a shared helper (e.g., reporting/markdown_renderers.py) and have both callers delegate to it. `ErrorAggregator._generate_markdown_report` and `generate_error_markdown` become one-line delegators or are removed.

findings.summary: output_validator.py reconstructs the source PDF path using identical multi-step logic in two separate functions (validate_copied_outputs and validate_output_structure), violating DRY at the abstraction level
evidence: output_validator.py:80-107 — `validate_copied_outputs` manually reconstructs repo_root and source_project_dir from path parts to find pre-copy PDF

output_validator.py:442-472 — `validate_output_structure` contains a near-identical block: same `path_parts`, same `repo_root = output_dir.parent.parent`, same `source_pdf_dir = repo_root / 'projects' / project_name / 'output' / 'pdf'`
The two blocks differ only in minor logging strings; the core path logic is copy-pasted
findings.suggestion: Extract `_resolve_source_pdf_path(output_dir: Path, project_name: str) -> Path | None` as a shared private helper that both functions call. This also makes the fallback heuristic testable in isolation.

findings.summary: infrastructure/reporting/suite_runner.py has no test file — critical path for pytest streaming and retry logic is untested
evidence: Glob for tests/infra_tests/reporting/test_suite_runner.py returns no files
Grep for 'suite_runner' in tests/infra_tests returns zero matches
suite_runner.py implements run_pytest_stream (binary non-blocking select loop) and run_test_suite (retry/coverage-conflict logic) — both are high-importer code paths
infrastructure/reporting/suite_runner.py is imported by scripts/01_run_tests.py (the primary test runner)
findings.suggestion: Add tests/infra_tests/reporting/test_suite_runner.py covering: run_test_suite with a trivially-passing pytest invocation in tmp_path, the coverage-conflict retry branch, and the check_test_failures halt path. Use real subprocess calls per the no-mocks policy.

findings.summary: test_environment.py docstring says 'Python 3.8+' but pyproject.toml requires >=3.10 — stale assertion documentation
evidence: test_environment.py:25: comment '# We're running on Python 3.8+, so this should pass'
pyproject.toml:32: requires-python = '>=3.10'
findings.suggestion: Update the inline comment to '# Python 3.10+ required per pyproject.toml' so it stays consistent with the declared constraint.

findings.summary: The logging subsystem is split into five peer files (logging_utils.py, logging_formatters.py, logging_progress.py, logging_helpers.py, logging_constants.py) inside infrastructure/core/ — a flat overload with 117 total importers pointing at logging_utils as the hub
evidence: Glob shows all five logging_*.py files live flat in infrastructure/core/ alongside 20+ unrelated modules
logging_utils.py has 135 importers (god module per holistic_context); it re-imports from the other four logging_* siblings
grep 'from infrastructure.core.logging_' shows 129 occurrences across 117 files — all routing through this flat namespace
The five files have clear internal cohesion (constants -> helpers -> formatters/progress -> utils) but no subdirectory boundary to make that hierarchy navigable
findings.suggestion: Move the five logging_*.py files into infrastructure/core/logging/ with an __init__.py that re-exports the public surface (get_logger, setup_logger, format_duration, etc.). This isolates the logging subsystem boundary without changing any external import paths that use logging_utils.

findings.summary: StreamingProgress.__init__ retains a multi-line Args docstring on a straightforward constructor that only assigns fields — verbosity not justified by complexity
evidence: logging_progress.py:228-237: StreamingProgress.__init__ has a 9-line docstring with Args section listing total/message/stream/update_interval for a constructor whose entire body is 5 field assignments
By contrast, Spinner.__init__ (same file, line 123) has no docstring at all — inconsistent verbosity within the same module
findings.suggestion: Replace the multi-line Args docstring on StreamingProgress.__init__ with a single-line summary or no docstring, matching the Spinner.__init__ convention in the same file.

findings.summary: Two independent code paths produce JSON+Markdown error reports with nearly identical structure
evidence: error_aggregator.py:198-222 ErrorAggregator.save_report() writes error_summary.json and error_summary.md
pipeline_reporter.py:451-485 save_error_summary() independently writes the same two files with the same categorization logic
Both functions produce by_type grouping and markdown formatting for the same error domain
findings.suggestion: Eliminate save_error_summary() in pipeline_reporter.py and route all error report generation through ErrorAggregator.save_report(); or remove ErrorAggregator.save_report() and delegate to the standalone function — pick one path and delete the other

findings.summary: Three-location PDF fallback search logic is implemented twice in output_validator.py
evidence: validate_copied_outputs() lines 36-111 implements: check root dir -> check pdf/ dir -> check source projects/ dir
validate_output_structure() lines 416-472 implements the same three-location fallback with near-identical path construction
Both functions independently construct repo_root via path_parts.index('output') and source_pdf_dir = repo_root / 'projects' / project_name / 'output' / 'pdf'
findings.suggestion: Extract a single _find_combined_pdf(output_dir, project_name) helper that returns the resolved Path or None; both validators call it and act on the result

findings.summary: validate_copied_outputs is a 136-line function mixing PDF search, directory iteration, and diagnostic logging
evidence: output_validator.py:16-171: single function performs project name extraction (lines 38-48), three-location PDF search (lines 49-125), directory presence loop (lines 127-169)
The three-location PDF search block alone spans 76 lines with nested conditionals
findings.suggestion: Extract _find_combined_pdf() for the search logic and _check_subdirectories() for the directory loop; validate_copied_outputs becomes a short compositor calling both

findings.summary: Blank line inside StreamingProgress._display before eta_str assignment is a residue of removed code
evidence: logging_progress.py:280-282: 'if self.current > 0 and elapsed > 0:' block ends, then blank line, then 'eta_str = f" | ETA: ..."' — the blank line suggests a deleted rate-limiting branch was not cleaned up
findings.suggestion: Remove the orphaned blank line at line 281 to restore control-flow clarity in _display

findings.summary: Module-level mutable singleton in error_aggregator.py is an implicit hidden dependency inconsistent with instance-based composition elsewhere
evidence: error_aggregator.py:291-303: _global_aggregator module-level variable with get_error_aggregator() lazy-init accessor
reset_error_aggregator() exists only to clear this global for testing, confirming it is a test-visible side effect
All other infrastructure state is passed as constructor arguments or function parameters
findings.suggestion: Remove the global singleton; callers that need a shared aggregator should construct one instance and pass it explicitly, or use a context object

findings.summary: pipeline_reporter.py combines data assembly, HTML generation, Markdown generation, JSON serialization, validation reporting, and error reporting in one module
evidence: pipeline_reporter.py exports: generate_pipeline_report (data assembly), save_pipeline_report (multi-format I/O), generate_html_report (HTML rendering), _generate_pipeline_markdown (MD rendering), generate_validation_report, generate_validation_markdown, generate_performance_report, save_error_summary, generate_error_markdown — 9 distinct responsibilities
generate_html_report is 150 lines of inline CSS + HTML f-string construction in a reporting data module
findings.suggestion: Split into pipeline_report_data.py (PipelineReport dataclass + generate_pipeline_report), pipeline_report_renderers.py (HTML/Markdown rendering functions), and keep save_* I/O thin wrappers; move validation and error report generation to their respective modules

findings.summary: ErrorAggregator.get_actionable_fixes() uses a string-keyed if/elif chain that should be data-driven
evidence: error_aggregator.py:136-194: explicit if error_type == 'test_failure' / elif error_type == 'validation_error' / elif error_type == 'stage_failure' / else pattern — adding a new error type requires editing this function
Each branch produces a structurally identical dict with priority, issue, actions, documentation keys
findings.suggestion: Define a dict mapping error type strings to (priority, actions, documentation) tuples; get_actionable_fixes() looks up the type and falls back to a generic entry, eliminating the branch chain
